what_it_does: This file is a draft noticeboard document that records a high-level interface-architecture proposal: make the interface layer typed, composed, and contract-governed, and route every requested change through a six-stage pipeline so changes are made correctly, documentation auto-updates, and duplication/drift are reduced.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board address, type=document, source=claude_code, state=draft, title, author_session, channel=dragnet-development, and an ordered list of linked board items.
History shows two transitions: null->draft (filed) and edit->order (interface architecture idea).
Body is a single short paragraph referencing 'Tim's drift concern' and proposing typed/composed/contract-governed interfaces plus a change pipeline.
The pipeline stages are listed inline: understand, locate, implement, validate, document, deploy-live.
It ends with the phrase 'Governs the fork.'
standouts: Proposes treating the interface layer as a typed, composed, contract-governed substrate.
Defines an explicit six-stage change pipeline (understand -> locate -> implement -> validate -> document -> deploy-live).
Frames the proposal as a direct response to 'Tim's drift concern' and as governing a fork.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter, id, address, type, state, history, and order fields.
Uses board:// URIs to reference related items in the order list.
Body is informal and telegraphic rather than structured; no sections, code, or concrete examples.
concerns: The body is a sketch and lacks concrete contract/type definitions or enforcement mechanism.
'Governs the fork' is asserted without specifying which fork or how governance is exercised.
No links populated in the links field despite an order list of related items.
It is in draft state, so completeness and approval status are unclear.
notes: A draft design note arguing that typed, contract-governed interfaces plus a rigid change pipeline will prevent drift and keep docs in sync.
questions: Who is Tim and what was the specific drift concern?
What concrete contracts, types, and composition rules are proposed?
How would the pipeline enforce documentation auto-update and detect duplication?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (interface architecture idea) matches the actual content, but the file is a high-level sketch rather than a detailed architecture specification.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a disciplined, repeatable change process for code/design interfaces so that every change is understood, correctly located, implemented, validated, documented, and deployed, thereby preventing divergence between implementation and documentation.
novelty: It treats the interface layer itself as a typed, composed, contract-governed company substrate and couples every change request to an explicit pipeline whose final stages auto-update documentation.
contribution: A concise framing of drift-proofing via typed interfaces and a six-stage change pipeline, useful as a design rationale or policy seed for how interface changes should be managed.
summary_for_embedding: A draft noticeboard item proposing typed, contract-governed interface architecture and a six-stage change pipeline to prevent drift and auto-update documentation.
intention_for_embedding: To capture an interface-architecture idea that answers a drift concern by enforcing a structured, documented change process.
